title: Agrostis elongata
taxonomy: Agrostis elongata is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of monocotyledonous plants that includes various types of grasses and other related species. Within the family Poaceae, Agrostis elongata is classified as part of the subfamily Pooideae, which is characterized by its distinctive leaf and flower structures. Further, Agrostis elongata is classified as part of the genus Agrostis, which includes several other species of grasses commonly found in a variety of habitats. The species Agrostis elongata is classified as a type of tall grass that can be found growing in temperate regions.

intro: Agrostis elongata, also known as redtop grass, is a perennial grass species native to North America. Traditionally, Agrostis elongata has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
